title: Google Sheets node serialization error
symptom: When executing Google Sheets nodes, the error 'dummy.stack.replace is not a function' intermittently appears. This issue can cause disruptions in workflow execution.
not_this_if: Using a Node.js version other than 18.x
Error does not occur with other nodes
root_cause: This error typically arises from a serialization problem with the error object in n8n's internal error handling. It is not directly related to the user's data or credentials but rather indicates a bug in the error handling mechanism, often exacerbated by specific Node.js versions or Docker image issues.
fix_steps: Check the Node.js version on your VPS and ensure it is 18.x (LTS).
If using Docker, pull the latest image for your version with 'docker pull n8nio/n8n:2.15.0'.
Consider upgrading n8n to at least version 2.17+ to benefit from patches addressing this issue.
As a temporary workaround, implement a scheduled workflow to refresh OAuth credentials periodically.
agent_instruction: Verify Node.js version and update n8n to at least version 2.17+.
